factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is improving, with a decrease in debt balances and a stable to slightly improving debt to capital ratio. The balance sheet is strengthening, supported by solid cash and marketable securities, strong operating cash flow, and access to credit facilities.

evidence.text: Our debt to capital ratio (total capital defined as debt plus equity) at July 4, 2021, was 30.9 percent, compared to 31.7 percent at December 31, 2020. The decrease was primarily due to $233 million of lower debt balances since December 31, 2020.

evidence.text: In July 2021, the Board of Directors (the Board) authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of 7.4 percent from $1.35 per share to $1.45 per share. In the first six months of 2021, we purchased $1,090 million, or 4.4 million shares of our common stock.

We made the following purchases under the 2019 stock repurchase program: • April 4: 1.7 million shares • July 4: 2.7 million shares Total: 4.4 million shares. In July 2021, the Board authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of 7.4 percent from $1.35 per share to $1.45 per share.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rate changes on the company is currently neutral to slightly positive. factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating across all operating segments and geographic regions, particularly in key markets like North America, China, and India, with strong performance in engine and distribution segments. The acceleration is supported by higher volumes, improved economic conditions, and recovery from the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Higher volumes and improved pricing, partially offset by higher overall compensation, higher premium freight costs due to supply chain constraints and rising commodity costs.

Worldwide revenues increased 59 percent in the three months ended July 4, 2021, compared to the same period in 2020, due to higher demand in all operating segments and all geographic regions due to an improved economic environment and lessening effects from the COVID-19 pandemic.

• We expect demand for pick-up trucks in North America to remain strong. • We estimate North American medium-duty and heavy-duty truck demand will continue to improve from 2020 levels. • We believe market demand for trucks in India will improve from 2020 levels.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, with consistent evidence across multiple subsections indicating successful price increases contributing to improved margins and revenue growth. However, these improvements are partially offset by rising costs such as higher compensation, premium freight, and commodity costs.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $588 million for the three months ended July 4, 2021 and increased 1.1 points as a percentage of sales, versus the comparable period in 2020. The increases in gross margin and gross margin as a percentage of sales were primarily due to higher volumes and improved pricing.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing strong positive volume trends across all segments, with significant increases in unit shipments across heavy-duty, medium-duty, and light-duty categories, as well as higher net sales driven by increased demand in key markets.

evidence.text: Unit shipments by engine classification were as follows: Heavy-duty 29,400 vs. 15,900, an 85% increase. Medium-duty 67,500 vs. 44,900, a 50% increase. Light-duty 68,100 vs. 29,800, a 127% increase. Total unit shipments 165,000 vs. 90,600, an 82% increase.

Net sales for the three months ended July 4, 2021, increased by $2,259 million versus the comparable period in 2020. The primary drivers were as follows: Engine segment sales increased 75 percent due to higher volumes in the North American heavy-duty truck and pick-up truck markets and global medium-duty truck markets.

Emission solutions sales increased $410 million primarily due to stronger demand in North America, Western Europe and India. Turbo technologies sales increased $135 million principally due to higher demand in North America and Western Europe.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by higher volumes and improved pricing across various segments. The growth is evident across all operating segments and geographic regions, with significant increases in demand attributed to an improved economic environment and reduced impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Engine segment sales for the three months ended July 4, 2021, increased $1,068 million versus the comparable period in 2020. The following were the primary drivers by market: Heavy-duty truck sales increased $424 million principally due to higher volumes in North America with shipments up 219 percent. Light-duty automotive sales increased $304 million primarily due to increased pick-up truck sales in North America with shipments up 272 percent. Medium-duty truck and bus sales increased $297 million mainly due to higher global medium-duty demand, especially in North America, Western Europe, Brazil and Mexico.

Worldwide revenues increased 38 percent in the six months ended July 4, 2021, compared to the same period in 2020, as we experienced higher demand in all operating segments and all geographic regions due to an improved economic environment and lessening effects from the COVID-19 pandemic.

Components segment sales for the three months ended July 4, 2021, increased $844 million versus the comparable period in 2020. The following were the primary drivers by business: • Emission solutions sales increased $410 million primarily due to stronger demand in North America, Western Europe and India.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum is predominantly positive, with significant growth observed across various regions and sectors, particularly in North America, Western Europe, and emerging markets such as China and India. The evidence highlights robust demand in automotive, heavy-duty truck, medium-duty truck and bus, construction, industrial, and power generation sectors, driven by economic recovery and increased shipments.

evidence.text: Heavy-duty truck sales increased $424 million principally due to higher volumes in North America with shipments up 219 percent.
Light-duty automotive sales increased $304 million primarily due to increased pick-up truck sales in North America with shipments up 272 percent.
Medium-duty truck and bus sales increased $297 million mainly due to higher global medium-duty demand, especially in North America, Western Europe, Brazil, and Mexico.

Worldwide revenues increased 59 percent in the three months ended July 4, 2021, compared to the same period in 2020, due to higher demand in all operating segments and all geographic regions due to an improved economic environment and lessening effects from the COVID-19 pandemic.
Power generation sales increased $231 million due to higher demand in North America, China and India.
Industrial sales increased $108 million due to stronger demand in global mining markets and higher demand in oil and gas markets in China.
